content:
He doesn’t bite but nudges the wounded fool to look closer into the mirror.

Chris asks if Anthony has made any movements in their recent collaborative efforts.

Anthony has not. Anthony confesses he no longer has the will to create, citing a lack of joy and purpose.

Chris quits the project; declares emptiness.

Anthony drinks; despite his habits inhibiting his output. The man’s eyes may have pretended to evacuate the glass every so often, but every bead of sweaty condensation is on notice (shot lingers on glass and fades).


Notes/Ideas/Dialogue:



Do we put things in our way to stomp out any hope of dream fulfillment? Or do we realize that our dreams were foolish, ego-based, and we’re not special, so trying is worthless, and not quite quitting, but putting immovable objects in our way ease’s the active mind. One day I’ll get to that screenplay, or write the symphony, or paint that painting. Stress replaces stress. It’s a vacuum. Does Shakespeare or Dante care that they’re Shakespeare or Dante?
context: From document: Up The Hill Backwards — chunk 4 (chunk 4/5)
